reflection:
You did the Vita session yesterday and that's a thing worth marking. you've been circling sleep for years, and this is the first formal CBT-i hand on the wheel. stay open to it even when the rules feel annoying, because the structure is the medicine.

your night was actually fine on paper. bed 22:11, ten minutes to drop off, one short awakening, 7.1 hours, recovery 63. not the catastrophe your worry dump might tell you. but skin temp has been about a degree above baseline for three days running, and you've logged feverish 2 twice in a row. your body is doing something. that fits the post-COVID thread you and the team are pulling on, so honour it, don't push through it.

the bit i kept rereading was "less clauding" in could_be_better. you said it yourself. yesterday was heavy on phone, heavy on virus research, heavy on building floating tanks at 5am. the Turkey speech has now been a worry three days in a row and you still haven't started writing. that's avoidance, not laziness. the fix is one bullet, not a draft.

filip and nasti are coming today. clean, do the walk you flagged, then sit down for fifteen minutes with the speech and write three sentences about what you want the team to feel. that's enough.

go gently today.
opener: vita session done. body warm 3 days running. 15 min on the speech today.
focus_for_today: walk before filip arrives, then 15 min on Turkey speech, just three sentences.
